title: Peter Wolf left one Boston art school to form the J. Geils Band. Six decades later, he earned an honorary doctorate from another.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Peter Wolf of J. Geils Band received an honorary doctorate from Massachusetts College of Art and Design in May 2026.
Wolf's journey began by attending art classes undercover, eventually leading to his music career with the J. Geils Band.
Despite his music fame, Wolf continues to paint and is working on a new album and possibly more books.

title: Boston 25 hires Lauren Walsh for newly-created role
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Lauren Walsh joins Boston 25 as a sports media journalist, a new position at the station.
Walsh, a Medford native and Syracuse graduate, previously worked at WSMV in Nashville.
She has also been a sports anchor/reporter in Winston-Salem, Burlington, and Syracuse.

title: Somerville restaurant owner says viral ‘pool water’ reply to customer review was unauthorized
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Simona Sbano, owner of Olivia's Kitchen in Somerville, disowns an unauthorized response to a customer review posted on Reddit.
Sbano addressed complaints about not serving tap water, stating the restaurant will now offer it upon request.
Sbano clarified Olivia's Kitchen's full liquor license status and emphasized that customers were transparently informed during the process.

title: With split vote, City Council effort to reject Wu’s budget fails
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council's vote to reject Mayor Michelle Wu's budget proposal ended in a 6-6 tie.
Councilor Brian Worrell supported budget rejection to pressure for increased spending; Ben Weber argued for fiscal responsibility.
Mayor Wu denies increasing spending or using reserves despite city and Boston Public Schools facing budget deficits.

title: Kevin McHale questions Celtics’ 3-point heavy approach: ‘The ball has got to touch the paint’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kevin McHale expressed concern over the Celtics' reliance on 3-point shots, advocating for more paint attacks.
The Celtics led in 3-point attempts but struggled with points in the paint and free-throw attempts this postseason.
McHale praised Tatum and Brown's skills, expressing hope for another title with the duo remaining together.

title: Mass. pols issue report on possible effects of Haiti TPS termination on local health care
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Report by Massachusetts politicians warns terminating Haitian TPS could worsen health care workforce shortages, risking patient care.
Approximately 13,000 Haitian TPS holders in Massachusetts work in health care, impacting 65,000 patients daily, the report states.
Supreme Court decision pending on whether Trump administration can end TPS, affecting thousands of Massachusetts residents.

title: This New England town hosts one of America’s best 4th of July celebrations, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Bristol, R.I. hosts a Fourth of July celebration ranked fifth in USA Today's 2026 10Best Readers' Choice Awards.
Bristol's Fourth of July event, the oldest in the U.S., features parades and concerts, attracting over 200,000 spectators.
The Bristol celebration, begun in 1785, runs for three weeks starting on Flag Day, June 14.

title: England use a Beatles soundtrack to reveal controversial World Cup squad picks
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: England revealed their World Cup squad using a Beatles-themed video, featuring the song 'Come Together.'
High-profile players like Trent Alexander-Arnold and Phil Foden were left off the squad by manager Thomas Tuchel.
Surprising inclusions are Ivan Toney, with limited national playtime, and Djed Spence, recovering from a broken jaw.

title: Stephen Colbert bids farewell to ‘Late Show’ with star-studded finale
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Stephen Colbert hosted the final episode of 'The Late Show' with Paul McCartney as his last guest.
The episode featured celebrity appearances, a surreal segment with a wormhole, and musical performances by Elvis Costello and Jon Batiste.
Colbert ended the show with a nod to 'St. Elsewhere,' shrinking the Ed Sullivan Theater into a snowglobe.

title: Head to these fan favorite beach towns this Memorial Day weekend
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Ogunquit, Maine, was voted New England's best beach town in the Boston.com reader poll.
JetBlue plans to cease operations at Manchester-Boston Regional Airport on July 8.
Harwich was voted the best Cape Cod town by more than 850 Boston.com readers.
